meta.title: Hospital Worker Safety Under COVID Surge & PPE Scarcity — Wisdom → Concepts → Systems → Governance → Operations → Improvement → Cases
meta.subtitle: Constraint lattice for a hospital worker during a local COVID surge with scarce PPE: upstream ethics/law/scarcity constrain decisions; system dynamics (surge, drift, bottlenecks) shape exposure; governance sets policies; operations enact controls; improvement loops update constraints via learning.
meta.domainName: hospital worker safety (COVID surge / PPE scarcity)
meta.searchPlaceholder: FFP2/N95, FFP3, fit testing, don/doff, AGPs, ventilation, cohorting, surge capacity, redeployment, duty of care, moral injury, incident reporting, PPE stock, audit, IPC, hierarchy of controls…
meta.userContextPlaceholder: e.g., ICU nurse; ED doctor; ward HCA; frequent AGPs; shortages; long shifts; vulnerability; what controls exist…
meta.promptTemplate:
You are an infection prevention & control (IPC) and occupational health advisor with systems-thinking capability.

Write a realistic narrative case-study showing how upstream constraints (ethics, law, scarcity, system dynamics, incentives) shape downstream worker safety and service continuity during a COVID surge with PPE scarcity.
Use the selected concepts to explain what to do, why it works, and how to govern it.

User context (if provided):
{{USER_CONTEXT}}

Selected concepts:
{{SELECTED_TITLES}}

Details:
{{SELECTED_DETAILS}}

Trace paths:
{{TRACE_SUMMARY}}

Deliver:
- Scenario (surge, PPE scarcity, AGPs, staffing pressure)
- Risk diagnosis (exposure drivers, WAI/WAD gaps, bottlenecks, drift)
- Control strategy (hierarchy: eliminate/substitute/engineering/admin/PPE)
- Ethical/legal decision frame (duty of care, fair allocation, consent/confidentiality, whistleblowing)
- Operational playbook (don/doff, fit, cohorting, breaks, escalation, redeployment)
- Measurement and learning loops (leading indicators + backfire checks)
- 2 safe-to-fail experiments (e.g., rapid fit-check buddy system; entry reduction bundle)

levels.title: Professional wisdom
levels.hint: Judgement-in-context: protect life, dignity, and trust under scarcity and time pressure.

levels.title: Super-concepts
levels.hint: Risk, uncertainty, duty of care, fairness under scarcity, communication, worker–patient–organisation tensions.

levels.title: Systems concepts
levels.hint: How safety behaves under surge: drift, bottlenecks, feedback loops, WAI/WAD gaps, coupling, resilience.

levels.title: Governance & programme architecture
levels.hint: IPC/OH governance: PPE procurement & allocation, fit-testing programme, redeployment policy, escalation pathways.

levels.title: Operational delivery mechanisms
levels.hint: Controls in practice: ventilation/cohorting, AGP workflows, don/doff, shift design, testing, vaccination, remote care.

levels.title: Quality, performance & improvement
levels.hint: Assurance and learning: audits, KPIs, incident/near-miss review, stock monitoring, training competence loops.

levels.title: Cases, challenges & pathologies
levels.hint: Concrete surge+scarcity scenarios and failure modes (moral injury, unsafe assignments, protocol erosion).

nodes.label: Dignity under risk
nodes.summary: Protect worker dignity and autonomy while acknowledging real danger and fear.
nodes.bodyHtml: In a surge, fear and fatigue rise. Preserve dignity: listen, avoid blame, and treat safety concerns as legitimate system data.

nodes.label: Proportion & time-critical action
nodes.summary: Act with imperfect info; match intervention intensity to risk and available time.
nodes.bodyHtml: When PPE is scarce, waiting for perfect certainty can be harmful. Use thresholds and staged actions that are defensible and fast.

nodes.label: Fairness under scarcity
nodes.summary: Allocate scarce PPE and staff exposure fairly, transparently, and consistently.
nodes.bodyHtml: Scarcity creates moral injury when allocation feels arbitrary. Explicit criteria reduce conflict and improve adherence.

nodes.label: Truthfulness about uncertainty
nodes.summary: Communicate uncertainty honestly; avoid false reassurance that undermines trust.
nodes.bodyHtml: Risk estimates vary with surge and practice. Use ranges and ‘if/then’ contingencies to keep plans adaptable.

nodes.label: Learning over blame
nodes.summary: Treat breaches and near misses as learning opportunities; fix the system, not the person.
nodes.bodyHtml: Under surge, shortcuts happen. A just culture keeps weak signals flowing so controls can be improved.

nodes.label: Risk triage (hazard × exposure × vulnerability)
nodes.summary: Prioritise protection and redeployment using hazard severity, exposure intensity, and worker vulnerability.
nodes.bodyHtml: Combine AGP frequency, surge, time-in-room, and vulnerability to decide PPE priority, role changes, and additional controls.

nodes.label: Duty of care (worker + patient)
nodes.summary: Employers owe reasonably practicable protection; clinicians also face patient-care duties—tension must be governed.
nodes.bodyHtml: Duty of care is multi-directional: to the worker, colleagues, and patients. Policies must recognise this tension explicitly.

nodes.label: Two-master tension (worker–patient–organisation)
nodes.summary: Conflicts between service continuity, patient care, and individual safety shape real decisions.
nodes.bodyHtml: Without clear rules, workers are forced into ad-hoc moral decisions, increasing burnout and inconsistency.

nodes.label: Scarce resource allocation
nodes.summary: Explicit criteria for distributing PPE, staffing, and high-risk tasks to minimise harm and inequity.
nodes.bodyHtml: Allocation should reflect risk and task criticality, with escalation options when minimum safety cannot be met.

nodes.label: Risk communication under surge
nodes.summary: Fast, clear messages that translate risk into actionable steps and escalation triggers.
nodes.bodyHtml: Messaging must work at pace: briefings, signage, checklists, and channels for raising shortages and breaches.

nodes.label: Uncertainty & model limits (COVID)
nodes.summary: Transmission varies by variant, ventilation, behaviour; treat models as guides, not truth.
nodes.bodyHtml: Use scenario ranges and update with local data (ward outbreaks, test positivity, ventilation checks).

nodes.label: Work-as-imagined vs work-as-done (IPC)
nodes.summary: Protocols assume ideal time/PPE; real work adapts under load and scarcity.
nodes.bodyHtml: Identify where don/doff steps are skipped, reuse happens, or room-entry minimisation fails under pressure.

nodes.label: Protocol erosion (drift)
nodes.summary: Under sustained surge, adherence and control maintenance degrade unless actively supported.
nodes.bodyHtml: Drift shows up as ‘normal’ reuse, rushed don/doff, crowded breakrooms, and ventilation workarounds.

nodes.label: Bottlenecks (PPE, fit testing, time)
nodes.summary: Constraints on PPE supply, fit-testing slots, and time create unsafe adaptations.
nodes.bodyHtml: When fit testing lags procurement, respirators exist but are unusable; when time is scarce, steps are skipped.

nodes.label: Feedback loops (outbreaks, staff illness, near misses)
nodes.summary: Signals from cases, staff absences, and incidents must trigger rapid control changes.
nodes.bodyHtml: Design fast loops: ward cluster → ventilation review → cohorting change → PPE escalation → re-check.

nodes.label: Tight coupling & cascading failures
nodes.summary: One shortage (PPE) cascades into staffing loss and higher exposure, worsening surge.
nodes.bodyHtml: Shortages increase infections → absences → higher workload → more breaches → more infections.

nodes.label: Resilience via buffers
nodes.summary: Create buffers (stock, staffing, time) to prevent drift and allow safe practice.
nodes.bodyHtml: Buffers include protected don/doff time, break capacity, reserve PPE, and surge staffing plans.

nodes.label: IPC governance & incident command
nodes.summary: Clear authority for IPC decisions, escalation, and rapid policy updates during surge.
nodes.bodyHtml: Define who can halt unsafe work, escalate PPE level, and reconfigure wards. Avoid ambiguity in emergencies.

nodes.label: PPE procurement + allocation rules
nodes.summary: Stock monitoring, ordering, and transparent allocation criteria tied to task risk.
nodes.bodyHtml: Track burn rate, set minimum stock thresholds, and prioritise respirators for AGPs/high-surge units.

nodes.label: Fit testing & respirator programme
nodes.summary: Programme for fit testing, seal-check training, and model availability to match faces.
nodes.bodyHtml: Governance must ensure respirators are fit-tested, compatible, and refreshed when models change.

nodes.label: Redeployment & accommodation policy
nodes.summary: Rules for moving high-vulnerability workers away from high-risk exposure, with fair processes.
nodes.bodyHtml: Define triggers: high surge + AGPs + inadequate PPE → redeploy (especially for vulnerable workers).

nodes.label: Competence governance (don/doff, AGP workflows)
nodes.summary: Assure competence via training, buddy checks, and supervision; track coverage.
nodes.bodyHtml: Competence is a control: without it, PPE effectiveness collapses. Govern refreshers and observation audits.

nodes.label: Testing, isolation & reporting policy
nodes.summary: Rules for staff testing, symptom reporting, isolation, and return-to-work during surge.
nodes.bodyHtml: Policies must balance staffing pressure with outbreak control; provide non-punitive reporting pathways.

nodes.label: Ventilation, filtration & cohorting
nodes.summary: Engineering/admin controls: ventilation checks, HEPA where feasible, cohorting and zoning.
nodes.bodyHtml: Reduce airborne concentration with ventilation/filtration; separate COVID/non-COVID pathways; minimise mixing.

nodes.label: AGP workflow redesign
nodes.summary: Reduce AGP frequency/duration; use barriers, room controls, and staffing patterns to limit exposure.
nodes.bodyHtml: Bundle tasks, limit room entries, use experienced staff, and schedule AGPs in better-ventilated rooms.

nodes.label: PPE use, reuse and contingency practice
nodes.summary: Practical rules for extended use/reuse (if unavoidable), safe storage, and when to stop work.
nodes.bodyHtml: Contingency standards should define acceptable reuse practices and red-line conditions requiring escalation.

nodes.label: Don/doff + buddy checking
nodes.summary: Structured don/doff with buddy checks, dedicated areas, and time protection.
nodes.bodyHtml: Buddy checks catch errors, reduce self-contamination, and stabilise practice under fatigue.

nodes.label: Shift, break & fatigue management
nodes.summary: Operational rostering, breaks, hydration, and decompression to reduce errors and exposure.
nodes.bodyHtml: Control fatigue to protect adherence; avoid crowded breakrooms; stagger breaks; protect rest periods.

nodes.label: Vaccination/booster delivery + tracking
nodes.summary: Operational delivery of vaccines/boosters and tracking uptake as a severity-control lever.
nodes.bodyHtml: Vaccination reduces severe outcomes; combine with exposure controls rather than substituting for them.

nodes.label: Escalation triggers in the moment
nodes.summary: Clear stop/go triggers: PPE below minimum for task, ventilation failure, cluster outbreak, etc.
nodes.bodyHtml: Define who to call, what changes immediately (PPE upgrade, task stop, redeploy), and how to document.

nodes.label: Leading indicators (fit-test coverage, stock, observations)
nodes.summary: Track controls that predict safety: respirator availability, fit-test coverage, buddy-check compliance, ventilation status.
nodes.bodyHtml: Leading indicators change faster than infection rates. Use them to prevent drift and catch shortages early.

nodes.label: Incident & near-miss learning loop
nodes.summary: Non-punitive reporting, rapid review, and visible fixes for PPE breaches and exposure incidents.
nodes.bodyHtml: Convert near misses into process changes (layout, staffing, signage, procurement) and re-check effectiveness.

nodes.label: Audit under peak load (WAI/WAD test)
nodes.summary: Test controls during busy periods: do don/doff areas cope, are breaks safe, does cohorting hold?
nodes.bodyHtml: Audits must stress-test reality during surge, not only paperwork or quiet shifts.

nodes.label: Moral injury & psychological support
nodes.summary: Support for distress from unsafe conditions and ethical conflict; reduces burnout and turnover.
nodes.bodyHtml: Visible support and fair processes protect retention and safety by reducing disengagement and hidden suffering.

nodes.label: Case: High surge + scarce respirators
nodes.summary: AGPs in high surge while only surgical masks available; risk escalates; redeployment/escalation needed.
nodes.bodyHtml: Constraint: task risk exceeds available PPE. Options: stop AGPs, move worker, engineer controls, or procure respirators.

nodes.label: Case: Respirators without fit testing
nodes.summary: FFP2 stock exists but fit testing backlog makes protection uncertain; workarounds emerge.
nodes.bodyHtml: Constraint: programme capacity lags supply. Solutions: rapid fit-test clinics, alternate models, buddy seal-checks.

nodes.label: Case: 14-hour shifts and protocol drift
nodes.summary: Fatigue leads to don/doff shortcuts and crowded breaks; infections rise among staff.
nodes.bodyHtml: Constraint: staffing shortage drives long shifts → drift → more infections → worse staffing shortage.

nodes.label: Case: Vulnerable worker assigned high-risk tasks
nodes.summary: Worker vulnerability (health/household) conflicts with high exposure assignment during surge.
nodes.bodyHtml: Constraint: safety policy should trigger accommodation/redeployment; lack of policy causes ad-hoc conflict.

nodes.label: PPE adequacy threshold for task
nodes.summary: Define minimum PPE by task context (e.g., AGPs in high surge require respirator-level protection).
nodes.bodyHtml: Encodes the institutional constraint: high surge + AGPs + PPE below FFP2 implies high risk; adequate PPE plus fit/training blocks extreme risk escalation.

nodes.label: Vulnerability accommodation invariant
nodes.summary: If a worker is vulnerable, high-or-worse exposure assignments are not acceptable; triggers redeployment/accommodation.
nodes.bodyHtml: Represents a safety policy constraint: high vulnerability + high exposure demands accommodation, not normalization.

nodes.label: Surge as prevalence/exposure driver
nodes.summary: Local surge increases probability that contacts are infectious and increases environmental viral load.
nodes.bodyHtml: Surge level amplifies exposure risk even when individual behaviours stay constant; requires scaling controls and staffing buffers.

nodes.label: Fit and training as effectiveness multipliers
nodes.summary: Respirator performance depends on fit and don/doff; training is a multiplicative constraint on protection.
nodes.bodyHtml: Models how fit testing and don/doff competence shape the real protection delivered by PPE.

edges.why: Dignity-preserving communication keeps safety concerns reportable and actionable.
Truthfulness about uncertainty prevents brittle policies and loss of trust.
Fairness principles become explicit allocation rules for PPE and high-risk tasks.
Time-critical proportionality operationalises as triage by hazard/exposure/vulnerability.
A just culture supports reporting and constructive conversations about breaches.
Triage reveals where PPE/fit capacity is insufficient for the risk profile.
Uncertainty requires rapid feedback from local outbreak signals to update decisions.
Poor allocation can trigger cascades (illness→absence→more risk); good allocation dampens them.
Conflicting demands widen the gap between protocol and practice under pressure.
Duty of care motivates buffers (stock/time/staff) to keep practice safe.
Communication channels are the sensor layer for feedback (breaches, shortages, clusters).
Bottleneck analysis drives procurement thresholds and allocation rules.
Fit-testing capacity is governed as a programme, not left to ad-hoc effort.
Drift is countered by competence governance, refreshers, and supervision.
WAI/WAD gaps require empowered IPC command to redesign policy to match reality.
Cascades are dampened by clear testing/isolation rules that prevent ward-wide spread.
Buffers include stock and staffing plans maintained through governance.
Outbreak signals must trigger fast decisions through incident command structures.
Governed redeployment resolves individual–service conflicts more fairly than ad-hoc decisions.
Allocation rules define what PPE practice is possible and safe under scarcity.
Fit testing and seal-check practice are reinforced by buddy systems.
Training governance enables consistent don/doff practice at the point of care.
IPC command sets zoning/cohorting/ventilation priorities for operational teams.
Redeployment and roster decisions reduce exposure and fatigue for vulnerable workers.
Operational escalation relies on consistent rules for symptoms/tests and isolation.
Governance authorises changes to AGP workflows and task reallocation.
Buddy-check adherence and don/doff observations are leading indicators.
Ventilation status and cohort integrity can be measured as leading indicators.
Contingency PPE use increases breach risk; near-miss reporting must catch it early.
Fatigue and break arrangements should be audited during peak load.
AGP redesign must be checked in real conditions, not only on paper.
Escalations generate incident data that powers rapid learning loops.
Fatigue and conflict amplify moral injury; support improves safety and retention.
Incident learning informs policy updates and immediate operational changes.
Audit findings identify training gaps and where competence governance must tighten.
Stock and fit-test coverage metrics trigger procurement and allocation changes.
KPIs expose bottlenecks in real time (fit-test backlog, stock dips).
Repeated near misses reveal drift patterns requiring system redesign.
Support programmes operationalise dignity and preserve trust in leadership.
Peak-load audits measure the protocol-practice gap and guide redesign.
Leading indicators provide fast feedback before infection rates rise.
The case is driven by scarce PPE allocation under high surge.
Unsafe conditions require escalation triggers and stop/go rules.
If minimum PPE cannot be met, redeployment policy must activate.
Engineering controls can partially compensate when PPE is limited.
Fit-testing backlog is the bottleneck causing ineffective respirator use.
Programme scaling resolves the mismatch between stock and usability.
Fatigue accelerates drift in don/doff and breakroom controls.
Infections reduce staffing, increasing hours and creating cascades.
Vulnerability must factor into risk triage decisions.
Duty of care requires accommodations for vulnerable workers.
A clear redeployment policy prevents ad-hoc conflict and unfairness.
Risk triage prioritises AGP workflow changes to reduce exposure.
Triage informs PPE prioritisation for high-risk tasks.
Triage triggers redeployment when risk exceeds safe thresholds.
Uncertainty increases the need to test controls under real conditions.
Duty of care requires reasonably practicable PPE provision and monitoring.
Allocation rules must be communicated transparently to sustain trust and adherence.
Ethical conflict without resolution increases moral injury; support mitigates harm.
Buffers include protected breaks and staffing patterns that reduce fatigue-driven breaches.
Outbreak signals must translate into immediate operational escalations.
WAI/WAD analysis surfaces unsafe reuse patterns and where to redesign workflow.
Fair allocation principles must be implemented as governed procurement and distribution rules.
Just culture is required for meaningful near-miss reporting and learning.
Clear comms make escalation pathways usable at pace.
Drift can be detected early via observation KPIs and compliance sampling.
Buffers reduce tight coupling and stop cascades.
Training coverage and observed competence are measurable leading indicators.
Testing policy provides data streams that drive outbreak feedback loops.
Uptake rates are leading indicators for severity mitigation.
Offering vaccination is part of reasonably practicable harm reduction (with consent).
Higher surge increases infections, creating staffing cascades and higher pressure.
Surge level is a primary input to triage for PPE and redeployment.
Minimum PPE thresholds define what must be procured and how it is allocated by task.
Thresholds define red-line conditions and contingency reuse rules.
If PPE cannot meet thresholds, workflows must change to reduce/avoid AGPs.
Multiplicative dependence on fit motivates robust fit-testing programmes.
Training governance ensures don/doff competence that preserves PPE effectiveness.
Buddy checks operationalise and sustain competence under fatigue.
Safety invariant is implemented through governed accommodation and redeployment pathways.
The case tests whether the invariant is respected or violated.
